Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
WHO WE ARE




TIFIN builds the AI operating layer for wealth. Our platform delivers agentic workflows across the industry’s core personas—investors, advisors, investment teams, and operations—so financial institutions can move faster, serve more clients, and deliver better outcomes with the same (or fewer) resources. We combine finance-native AI, specialized data, and enterprise-grade controls to deploy secure, compliant capabilities into real production environments.




WHAT SETS US APART




Speed: We build and ship quickly—MVPs in ~3 months, production-ready products in ~6–12 months.
Track Record: Prior exits include 55ip (acquired by J.P. Morgan) and Paralel
Strategic Partners: Partners include J.P. Morgan, SEI, Franklin Templeton, Morningstar, Broadridge, Motive Partners and Tectonic Ventures.
World-Class Team: Complimentary expertise across AI and financial services, with experience from Google, Microsoft, Uber, PayPal, eBay, BlackRock, LPL, Franklin Templeton, Morgan Stanley, Broadridge and more.




OUR VALUES




Grow at the Edge. We are driven by personal growth fueled by a beginner’s mindset. We get out of our comfort zone and keep egos aside. With self-awareness and integrity we strive to be the best we can possibly be. No excuses.
Understanding through Listening and Speaking the Truth. We communicate with authenticity, precision and integrity to create a shared understanding. We identify opportunities within constraints and propose solutions in service to the team.
I Win for Teamwin. We believe in staying within our genius zones to succeed and taking accountability for driving results. We are all individual contributors first and always thinking about what can be better.




ROLE OVERVIEW




As a Senior Data Scientist, you will build and deploy the ML growth engine behind TIFIN AMP — a production machine learning platform driving acquisition, expansion, retention, and distribution intelligence across wealth enterprises and asset managers. This is an individual contributor role with close collaboration with engineering and customer success.




PROJECTS




Client Retention & Churn Prevention. Build early-warning models that detect signals of potential client attrition, allowing advisors to proactively intervene.
Asset Consolidation. Develop predictive models that identify clients most likely to consolidate additional assets with the firm, enabling advisors to proactively deepen relationships and increase assets under management.
Cross-Sell & Wallet Share Growth. Design models that identify the highest-propensity opportunities for additional products and services within existing client relationships.
Advisor & Prospect Prioritization. Create scoring frameworks that rank advisors and prospects by growth potential, enabling sales teams to focus on the most impactful opportunities.
Product–Advisor Matching. Develop models that predict product–advisor fit and likelihood of engagement, improving targeting and accelerating product adoption.




WHAT YOU’LL DO




Own the full ML lifecycle: EDA → feature engineering → training → validation → deployment → monitoring
Own end to end model implementation for one or more enterprise clients
Engineer large-scale feature pipelines across AUM, transactions, CRM, and third-party datasets
Deploy models using AWS (SageMaker, Redshift, S3) and/or Snowflake
Design A/B tests and control frameworks to measure lift in inflows and conversion
Translate model outputs into clear, actionable intelligence used daily by advisors and wholesalers




WHAT YOU’LL BRING




4+ years shipping production ML systems
Strong supervised learning experience
Experience modeling consumer behavior, flows, or lifecycle dynamics
Python, SQL, DBT; AWS or Snowflake experience
Bias toward shipping measurable impact




COMPENSATION RANGE




$150,000 - $190,000 USD




In addition to cash compensation, a meaningful equity stake is a significant part of the overall package. Package also includes benefits program eligibility: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits and flexible PTO.




TIFIN is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and values the multitude of talents and perspectives that a diverse workforce brings. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or protected veteran status.




Please see more details on our privacy practices in our Privacy Notice here.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Our client is a top financial institution with significant North American holdings. They have operations across most major verticals, including institutional & corporate, wealth management, private clients, commercial banking, treasury, and retail banking.
 
Introduction:
Robertson is seeking a skilled Data Scientist to join our client in support of an existing vacancy.

Contract Period: 6 months      
Pay Rate: Starting from $64 per hour
Location: Toronto, ON
Location Type: Hybrid
Business Hours: Monday-Friday, Core business hours
 
Job Responsibilities:
Prepare, clean, and analyze datasets for ML/AI features from complex and fragmented internal data sources
Leverage LLMs to create features from unstructured data
Design and build segmentation and predictive models for customer and advisor analytics
Own feature engineering pipeline for ML/AI models
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand workflows, data requirements, and key performance metrics
Build dashboards and reporting assets to serve insights to business stakeholders
Contribute to development and evaluation of modular Gen AI features (RAG systems, NL-to-SQL, agentic workflows)
Develop and implement analytics enabled solutions that support business goals and process improvement
Deliver complete projects of moderate complexity
Translate analytical findings into business language and recommend solutions to stakeholders and leadership
Document data sources, contribute to structured processes, and support closed loop tracking for continuous improvement

Experience & Qualification Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Math, Computer Science, Engineering required
3-5 years of technical experience
Strong Python skills, including experience with data science libraries (e.g., pandas, NumPy, scikit learn, PySpark or similar)
Strong SQL experience and proficiency with data modeling concepts
Proficiency with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms
Demonstrated experience engineering complex features from large, messy, and multi source datasets and to assess feature quality
Demonstrated experience in end-to-end model development: problem framing, data preparation, feature engineering, model training, validation, and deployment support
Experience with classical statistical methods and ML techniques (e.g., regression, clustering, PCA, decision trees, survival analysis)
Ability to translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical approaches
Curiosity about GenAI and eagerness to learn LLM related workflows, evaluation techniques, and best practices
Ability to communicate insights clearly to business partners and contribute to solution ideation within broader business strategy
How to Apply: If you are a motivated professional looking to contribute to a leading team, please submit your resume outlining your qualifications and experience relevant to this role. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status, Aboriginal status, or any other legally protected factors. We champion building a diverse and inclusive environment.

Title: Senior AI/ML Lead
Description:
About RentFlow

RentFlow is tackling a hair-on-fire problem for small and medium businesses across the U.S.: the way their rent is paid each month is fundamentally misaligned with how cash actually comes in.

We’re building the infrastructure layer to fix that.

Today, ~50% of U.S. businesses are late on rent in any given month. The rest face invisible struggles to pay on time, undermining their stability month after month.

The core issue is structural: rent hits as a single fixed lump sum on the 1st, stacked on top of every other major monthly bill, while revenue and other expenses flow in unevenly. This forces healthy businesses into recurring cash-flow stress and is a major driver of business closures.

RentFlow is the first startup to turn fixed business rent into a cash-flow-synced product, underwritten in real time from transaction-level data to prevent issues before they even surface.

This opens up a $20B+ rent-splitting market and creates a natural wedge into an AI CFO layer for businesses and data opportunities we're uniquely positioned to own.

We're seed-stage, live since May 2025, and growing ~3x MoM. We're ramping up partnerships with the leading rent payment platforms in the U.S., fast-growing SMB software companies, and major property players.

Early-Stage Opportunity

We’re hiring our AI/ML lead to own underwriting, cash-flow intelligence, and data insights end-to-end.

This is a foundational role. You’ll design and ship models that make real financial decisions in production, affecting how and when business owners pay rent each month. You’ll work directly with the founders and have meaningful ownership over both technical direction and business outcomes.

What You’ll Do


Model messy, real-world SMB cash flows from transaction-level data
Build and iterate on underwriting & decisioning systems
Leverage LLMs to surface unique insights into business owner behavior and the forces shaping cash flow beneath the surface
Turn raw financial data into clear, natural insights for business owners and their partners
Design signals that balance risk, growth, and user trust in real time
Collaborate closely with founders on product, risk, and roadmap decisions
Shape the long-term architecture of RentFlow’s AI & data stack


You’ll Be a Great Fit If You


Have built and shipped ML or data-driven systems in production
Have worked with LLMs in production and know where to apply them
Are comfortable working with imperfect, noisy financial data
Care about real-world impact
Enjoy high ownership, fast iteration, and ambiguous problem spaces
Want to help define the technical DNA of an early-stage company
Are excited to be an early hire with outsized impact, real equity, and real leverage on product and company direction


Why This Role Is Different


Your models directly control real money and real outcomes
You’ll help define an entirely new category at the intersection of rent, finance, and AI
You’ll work side-by-side with founders
You’ll have meaningful equity and real leverage from day one


If you want to build something with real stakes, real users, and real impact as an early hire, we’d love to talk!

Title: Data Analyst, Mexico City [12-month Fixed Term Contract]
Description:
LinkedIn is the world’s largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We’re also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that’s built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed.



Join us to transform the way the world works.





Job Description



At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team.



This role is part of LinkedIn’s broader effort to build and scale an in-house owned media agency, including the creation of tools that will significantly increase production speed and operational efficiency. As such, the position is structured as a 12-month contractor role. This role will be based in Mexico City, Mexico.



Job Overview



The Owned In-House Agency Team supports the LinkedIn Owned Media team, which leads channel strategy, prioritization, and performance across Email and LinkedIn Ads. This team partners closely with Owned Media to bring campaigns to life at scale, ensuring high-quality execution and operational excellence across a complex portfolio. This includes driving execution of campaign builds, QA, and reporting insights based on the Owned Media team's strategy and priorities. This team also plays a key role in enabling efficiency and consistency through the use and enablement of technology, automation, and tooling, delivering impact through execution, scalability, and continuous improvement within a high-performing marketing organization.



This role is responsible for executing day-to-day performance tracking, reporting, and analysis across Owned marketing channels. Working closely with internal stakeholders, this role supports visibility into channel performance by maintaining dashboards, analyzing trends, and ensuring data accuracy. The analyst will ensure accurate and timely reporting, and will play a key role in surfacing actionable insights and supporting optimization efforts to improve overall marketing effectiveness.



If you are passionate about leveraging data to drive business decisions and thrive in a dynamic, global environment, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.



Responsibilities



Build, maintain, and update performance dashboards and recurring reports across Owned channels
Ensure accuracy and consistency of data across reporting tools, spreadsheets, and dashboards
Monitor channel performance and proactively flag trends, anomalies, or areas of concern
Analyze campaign and channel metrics to provide actionable insights and optimization recommendations
Support stakeholders by delivering timely performance readouts and answering ad hoc data requests
Track and troubleshoot data issues across Owned tools and platforms, escalating when needed
Partner with cross-functional teams to ensure proper data tracking, tagging, and reporting setup
Assist in documenting reporting processes and maintaining data governance best practices
Support implementation and QA of automated reporting solutions and analytics tools


Qualifications



Basic Qualifications



BA/BS degree or equivalent work experience.
Minimum of 4 years’ experience in an analytics role
Business fluency in English
Proven ability to translate business challenges into analytical frameworks.
Confident in managing stakeholder relationships and working independently.
Experience with SQL and BI tools such as Tableau and Power BI.



Preferred Qualifications



Demonstratable experience of AI/ML concepts and their application in data analysis.





Skills



Performance Analytics
Dashboarding & Reporting
Data Accuracy & Integrity
Marketing Monitoring & Optimization
Cross-Functional Collaboration


Additional Information



Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates ​



Please follow this link to access the document that provides transparency around the way in which LinkedIn handles personal data of employees and job applicants: https://legal.linkedin.com/candidate-portal.

Title: Machine Learning Ops Engineer | Remote | $90 –$140/hr
Description:
About The Role

This role focuses on advancing next-generation AI systems through large-scale ML infrastructure, training optimization, and framework-level engineering. The work involves supporting cutting-edge GenAI initiatives, improving model performance, and contributing to highly scalable AI training environments.

Position: MLOps Engineer

Type: W2 | Full-Time Contingent Role

Engagement: Remnote Global | Full-time

Compensation: $90–$140/hour

Location: United States (Remote)

Role Responsibilities

Support AI research and engineering teams in improving ML infrastructure and training systems
Design advanced MLOps and ML systems tasks with accurate, structured technical solutions
Evaluate ML systems outputs and provide detailed technical feedback
Develop evaluation rubrics and frameworks for distributed systems, training pipelines, and kernel-level optimization
Collaborate with domain experts to maintain consistency and quality across AI training workflows
Contribute to improvements in large-scale model training performance and infrastructure reliability

Requirements

2+ years of professional experience in ML infrastructure, MLOps, or ML systems engineering
Hands-on production experience with JAX and/or PyTorch at scale
Experience writing or optimizing GPU kernels using Pallas or Triton
Strong understanding of ML training systems and distributed infrastructure
Demonstrated career progression in engineering or AI infrastructure roles
Ability to commit to a full-time 40-hour/week weekday schedule
Strong written communication and technical documentation skills

Engagement Details

W2 employment engagement
Full-time, 40 hours/week
No conflicting full-time engagements permitted
Remote role within the United States
Opportunity to contribute to leading frontier AI initiatives

Application & Onboarding Process

Upload resume
AI interview: A short, 15-minute conversational session to assess background and technical expertise
Follow-up communication with next steps and onboarding details

Flexible remote contract supporting advanced large language model development initiatives

Work across disciplines including Healthcare, Statistics, Data Science, ML Research, Computer Science, Game Development, and Mechanical/Aerospace Engineering
Collaborate on high-impact model training and evaluation projects with frontier research teams
Independent contractor engagement with flexible scheduling and project-based continuity
Weekly payouts via Stripe or Wise

Apply deep expertise in fields such as Medicine, AI/ML, Data Science, Computer Science, Game Development, or Engineering
Demonstrate strong analytical thinking and exceptional attention to technical accuracy and detail

Contribute independently in a fast-paced remote research environment while following structured evaluation guidelines

Title: Python Engineer
Description:
About The Company

Outlier AI is a leading technology firm dedicated to transforming the landscape of artificial intelligence and data analytics. Our innovative solutions empower organizations across various industries to harness the full potential of their data, driving smarter decision-making and operational efficiencies. Committed to excellence and continuous improvement, Outlier AI fosters a dynamic and collaborative environment where talent and innovation thrive. Our team of experts specializes in developing cutting-edge AI models, data processing tools, and scalable platforms that meet the evolving needs of our clients worldwide.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Data Scientist to join our dynamic team at Outlier AI. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and implementing advanced data models and algorithms that address complex business challenges. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including product development, engineering, and business stakeholders to translate data insights into actionable strategies. The ideal candidate will possess a strong analytical mindset, proficiency in machine learning techniques, and a passion for leveraging data to create impactful solutions. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work on innovative projects that shape the future of AI-driven technology and make a tangible difference in various sectors.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field; Ph.D. is a plus.
Proven experience in data analysis, machine learning, and statistical modeling.
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, or Java.
Experience with data visualization tools and libraries like Tableau, Power BI, or matplotlib.
Strong understanding of database systems and query languages such as SQL.
Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and within a team.
Effective communication skills to present complex data insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders.


Responsibilities


Design, develop, and optimize machine learning models and algorithms to solve real-world problems.
Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
Collaborate with product teams to integrate AI solutions into existing platforms and workflows.
Conduct experiments and validate models to ensure accuracy, robustness, and scalability.
Stay updated with the latest advancements in AI and data science to continuously improve our offerings.
Document methodologies, findings, and recommendations clearly and effectively.
Participate in code reviews, team meetings, and knowledge-sharing sessions to foster a collaborative environment.


Benefits


Competitive salary package with performance-based incentives.
Comprehensive health insurance plans covering medical, dental, and vision care.
Opportunities for professional growth and continuous learning through training and conferences.
Flexible work hours and remote work options to promote work-life balance.
Generous paid time off and holiday leave policies.
A vibrant and inclusive company culture that values diversity and innovation.
Access to cutting-edge tools and resources to support your work and development.


Equal Opportunity

Outlier AI is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all employees. We celebrate diversity and are dedicated to ensuring equal employment opportunities regardless of race, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law. We believe that diverse teams drive innovation and creativity, and we strive to create a workplace where everyone can thrive and contribute to our shared success.

Title: Head of ML/AI Exploration, Architecture, & Engineering
Description:
About The Company

X is Alphabet’s moonshot factory where uncomfortably ambitious, world-changing ideas are developed and taken out into the world. X projects have the potential to transform the lives of billions of people and make the world a radically better place. For X to be successful, we have to repeatedly turn breakthrough technology ideas into products that solve large problems in the world, and create meaningful businesses for Alphabet.

About The Team

X’s central Applied AI team is looking for a leader motivated by a desire to make a difference in the world. You will apply your expertise to diverse X projects, tackling domain-specific problems with cutting-edge AI.

About The Role

As the Head of the Applied AI team, your work is to discover, invent, solve, and prototype. From devising experiments and building robust implementations to designing new algorithms, You’ll lead a small team of Research Scientists and work on applying artificial intelligence to critically important, often ambiguous, problems. Our team values individuals who can bridge the gap between innovative research and practical, impactful solutions.

How You Will Make 10x Impact


Attract and develop a diverse team of ML/AI experts, as well as guide and coach them on all aspects of their research. Foster a collaborative environment for brainstorming, advising, and knowledge sharing.
Partner on the technical vision and strategy for our different early stage and early investigation projects; co-design and co-lead a technical roadmap.
Develop, implement, and deploy robust, scalable ML/AI solutions for real-world problems across multiple X projects and scientific domains, utilizing state of the art developments and techniques with a focus on foundation models and transformer architectures.
Design and build hybrid AI models, potentially integrating LLMs with other tools and techniques (e.g., graph-based ML, reinforcement learning).
Provide research and engineering leadership on X early explorations: defining problem scope, identifying optimal AI/ML approaches, and evaluating their merits.
Guide data strategy and contribute to ML pipeline development and system evaluation.
Contribute to code reviews, establish best practices for ML engineering, and optimize code for performance and scalability.
Mentor and guide other team members, act as X’s link to the broader research and academic community, as well as to Alphabet’s other research groups, staying abreast of and integrating the latest advancements.


What You Should Have


PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related technical field.
Experience leading a team of research scientists and in mentoring and guiding junior researchers or engineers.
Experience in applying ML/AI techniques to complex problems.
Strong, hands-on experience with LLMs/Transformers, including their theoretical underpinnings and practical application in post-training (e.g., fine-tuning, RLHF, prompting, agent development).
Experience deploying backend LLM applications.
Proficiency in Python and deep experience with ML/Deep Learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, JAX, HuggingFace, LangChain).


It’d Be Great If You Also Had These


Experience with graph-based machine learning techniques.
Meaningful experience or a strong understanding of applying AI/ML in another scientific discipline such as biology, psychology, neuroscience, or a related field.
Demonstrable research experience, evidenced by contributions to projects, and ideally through publications in relevant ML/NLP venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, EMNLP).
Industry experience in a startup or fast-paced R&D environment.


The US base salary range for this full-time position is $307,000 - $422,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits.

An Equal Opportunity Workplace

At X, we don't just accept difference - we celebrate it, we support it, and we thrive on it for the benefit of our employees, our products and our community. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements.

If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please contact us at x-accommodation-request@x.team.

Title: Plant Biologist
Description:
Job Title: Plant Biologist




Job Type: Contract




Location: Remote




Job Summary:

Join our customer's team as a Plant Biologist and play a pivotal role in advancing AI training by researching plant biology at the molecular and cellular level. Utilize your expertise to drive innovation in agriculture, pharmaceuticals, biofuels, and environmental sciences. This expert-level, remote opportunity is ideal for those passionate about the integration of biological sciences and cutting-edge technology.




Key Responsibilities:

Conduct detailed research on plant biology, genetics, and cellular processes for AI training purposes.
Collect, analyze, and document plant samples from diverse environments.
Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to translate biological insights into actionable data for AI models.
Design and implement experiments to investigate plant responses to various environmental factors.
Integrate principles of biology, chemistry, and geology to enhance data accuracy and project outcomes.
Prepare high-quality written and verbal communications to articulate findings and recommendations.
Contribute to the development of datasets and frameworks for AI applications in life sciences.




Required Skills and Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Biology, Chemistry, or a related field (Master’s or PhD preferred for advanced research roles).
Demonstrated expertise in plant biology, genetics, and molecular techniques.
Strong foundations in sample collecting, data analysis, and experimental design.
Proficiency in integrating cross-disciplinary knowledge from chemistry and geology.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a focus on scientific reporting.
Experience working in laboratory settings and with relevant research equipment.
Ability to work independently and as part of a distributed, remote team.




Preferred Qualifications:

Rubric experience or familiarity with data annotation for AI training.
Prior experience contributing to projects at the intersection of biology and artificial intelligence.
Published research in peer-reviewed journals or contributions to scientific datasets.

Title: Quality Assurance Engineer
Description:
Aviture is seeking an experienced quality assurance engineer to be part of a cross functional software development team. Aviture provides effective software solutions through an Agile mindset and a high degree of team empowerment. This position provides significant opportunity for collaboration and growth alongside other outstanding engineers spanning a wide variety of backgrounds.

Requirements

The expectations for a candidate applying for this position are as follows:


Requires a deep understanding of software QA methodologies, tools and processes
Must possess an astute understanding of the Agile manifesto and Scrum development framework
Will work with the team to determine product quality and release readiness standards
Must have significant experience in the creation and management of automated test suites
Must demonstrate the ability to work with various QA/Testing tools and technologies. (JIRA, Azure DevOps, Appium, Azure TestPlans, etc...)
Experience with Web, Web Service/API, and Mobile software applications testing required
Expected to research and analyze the product features being tested
Expected to actively participate as a team member in the elaboration of user stories, and associated acceptance criteria
Expected to review User Interfaces for consistency and functionality
Responsible for the creation and execution of test plans with the goal of discovering bugs/quality issues within the software
Responsible for recording test progress and results, as well as assist in analyzing the testing metrics to determine systemic issues, trends, categorization of escaped defects, etc.
Must demonstrate the ability to cross-pollinate and instill a ‘test infected' mindset upon the development team.
Must perform generalized tasks as needed to work as a part of a cohesive team towards achieving Sprint goals
Must have knowledge of the current trends and latest advancements in software QA
Will work to drive innovation and streamline the overall testing process within the team and the company at large
Will assist in the creation of company-wide guidance and policy concerning quality assurance
Must demonstrate significant initiative, persistence and the ability to work well as part of an empowered delivery team
Must have strong communication skills, with the ability to interact with both technical and non-technical resources


Benefits

Learn more about Aviture at www.aviture.us.com


PPO or a High Deductible Health Plan, HSA eligible plan, Dental, Vision, and Supplemental Life.
Generous paid time off that starts accruing on day one
Hybrid work environment with quarterly Castaway Week events in the Omaha HQ Office, all expenses paid, from across the country to participate in team-building, collaboration, and fun events
Company vacations!!! Previous destinations have included Napa Valley, Cancun, Cabo San Lucas and Aspen
Matching 401k with 100% vesting in six months
Pick your preferred workstation! Mac? PC? We'll support you with the tech you need to do your best work
Get paid to go to national and international conferences and training, so that you can stay up to date with the latest innovations
Flexible work schedules: Work the times that fits your schedule, with all meetings required to be scheduled between 9 am and 3 pm CST
Free lunch every Friday and recurring team events for you and your family to enjoy
Incentive and bonus pay.
Opportunities for connection and growth through peer coaching, recurring Lightning Talks to share things we're passionate about, and a bi-weekly company-wide All Hands

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
We're hiring on behalf of a Haystack partner!




The Role

• Define, develop, document, and own scalable solution blueprints and delivery playbooks for GenAI/Hybrid-AI deployments on dedicated on-premises hardware.

• Collaborate closely with customers, partners, product management, and engineering teams to tailor solutions to specific vertical requirements and constraints.

• Design and develop reference architectures and proofs-of-concept, optimizing models and pipelines for performance, latency, accuracy, and cost.

• Integrate and apply advanced AI models including LLMs, VLMs, VLAs, and computer vision stacks.

• Architect and implement production-grade patterns for video analytics, enterprise GenAI (e.g., RAG, copilots), and multimodal agents interacting with enterprise systems.

• Design hybrid AI architectures, partitioning workloads across edge devices, on-premises appliances, and cloud environments.




What You'll Need

• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related field, with significant years of experience in Systems Engineering or AI/ML.

• Proven track record architecting and shipping complex, systems-level AI solutions that consider application, runtime, and platform factors.

• Deep hands-on proficiency with AI model lifecycles, including selection, fine-tuning, compression, quantization, and hardware-aware optimization.

• Expertise in designing hybrid AI strategies and strong software engineering foundations (Python/C++), containerization, and inference/runtime stacks.

• Customer-facing experience launching new AI products and driving POCs to production with clear KPIs.

• Excellent communication and leadership skills to influence across teams and engage with customers and executives.




What's On Offer

• A competitive salary in the range of $220,200 - $330,400.

• Opportunity to work on pioneering on-premises AI appliance technology.

• Be part of a fast-growing business unit at the forefront of AI and edge computing innovation.

• Comprehensive benefits package designed to support personal and professional success.




Apply via Haystack today!

• Expertise in designing hybrid AI strategies and strong software engineering foundations (Python/C++), containerization, and inference/runtime stacks.

• Customer-facing experience launching new AI products and driving POCs to production with clear KPIs.

• Excellent communication and leadership skills to influence across teams and engage with customers and executives.


What's On Offer

• A competitive salary in the range of $220,200 - $330,400.

• Opportunity to work on pioneering on-premises AI appliance technology.

• Be part of a fast-growing business unit at the forefront of AI and edge computing innovation.

• Comprehensive benefits package designed to support personal and professional success.


Apply via Haystack today!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Samba is a media intelligence company. We know what the world is watching, reading, and thinking about — in real time, at scale, across every screen. Our data exists with the consent of over a billion people, organized into the most complete picture of consumer attention ever built. The biggest brands in the world use that picture to make smarter decisions. We think it’s the most interesting data asset on the planet, because it’s the most culturally relevant. 




As a mid-level Data Scientist at Samba in Warsaw, you will own end-to-end delivery of significant data science projects with minimal guidance. You are a reliable, autonomous contributor with deep expertise in at least one of Samba's core domains — measurement, or audience modelling — and the technical range to build production-ready solutions using modern ML and AI methodologies. You'll work closely with peers, product, and engineering, and play an active role in mentoring junior data scientists on the team.




What You'll Do:


Own end-to-end delivery of significant data science projects — from problem scoping and approach design through to production deployment


Make sound, independently-reasoned decisions on methodology, model selection, and evaluation; document them clearly in technical solution documents covering problem statement, approach, metrics, and timeline


Lead solution design for your own initiatives; break down complex epics into well-scoped user stories with clear acceptance criteria, adopting DataOps and MLOps best practices throughout — experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration, model monitoring, and reproducibility


Build production-quality Python and PySpark code on Databricks — well-tested, documented, and reusable — and implement advanced ML and AI-powered workflows including entity resolution, probabilistic record linkage, embedding-based matching, semantic similarity, and LLM-augmented pipelines


Develop and maintain reusable tools, libraries, and documentation that improve team efficiency and technical standards; conduct code reviews with constructive, specific feedback that raises the bar


Mentor junior data scientists on technical execution, code quality, and career development; lead internal talks or workshops on ML topics


Collaborate cross-functionally with product, engineering, and operations — translate business requirements into technical specifications, partner with data engineering on scalable pipeline design, and participate in cross-functional design reviews and working groups






Who You Are:


Bachelor's degree required in Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics or a related quantitative field; Master's strongly preferred


3-5 years of hands-on data science experience with demonstrated ability to own and deliver complex, multi-sprint projects independently


Advanced Python with production-quality code, testing, and documentation; strong SQL and PySpark for billion-row datasets


Databricks workflows, Delta Lake, and job orchestration; working knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS or GCP)


Solid command of core ML — regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, and experimental design — applied to complex, high-volume data


Proficiency with MLOps practices: experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration (Airflow), and reproducible model deployment


Exposure to modern AI methodologies: RAG systems, LLM-augmented models, vector databases, and semantic search


Strong communicator — able to translate technical work into clear documentation, user stories, and cross-functional conversations


Demonstrated ability to mentor junior data scientists and contribute to team standards






Preferred skills:


Hands-on experience with knowledge graph construction, entity resolution, or semantic data modeling (RDF, OWL, SPARQL, or equivalent graph frameworks)


Familiarity with probabilistic record linkage, identity graph approaches, or embedding-based entity matching at scale


Experience with causal inference methods (A/B testing, synthetic control, uplift modeling)


Experience with deduplication, enrichment, or web-to-TV linkage problems


Background in media, ad tech, or measurement — TV viewership (ACR/STB data), digital audience modeling, cross-platform measurement (linear + CTV/OTT), or identity resolution in privacy-constrained environments


Familiarity with the measurement and identity vendor landscape (Nielsen, Comscore, LiveRamp, The Trade Desk









Samba is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.  We strive to empower connection with one another, reflect the communities we serve, and tackle meaningful projects that make a real impact.




 




Samba may collect personal information directly from you, as a job applicant, Samba may also receive personal information from third parties, for example, in connection with a background, employment or reference check, in accordance with the applicable law. For further details, please see Samba's Applicant Privacy Policy. For residents of the EU , Samba Inc. is the data controller.




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Science Specialist
Description:
Job description:



Founded in 1913 in Italy, the Prada Group was built on a tradition of excellence and with a vision of innovation. The Group, a world leader in the luxury sector, operates in more than 45 countries with the PRADA, Miu Miu, Versace, Church’s, Car Shoe and Luna Rossa brands, and has employees of over 100 nationalities.

 

 

JOB PURPOSE


We are seeking a Data Science Specialist to join the Data & Analytics Team within IT. Based in our Milan HQ, the role focuses on designing and industrializing AI solutions for customer personalization and recommendation systems. The ideal candidate combines strong technical skills with the ability to coordinate small external development teams and work across multiple IT and business functions on complex enterprise programs.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES

 

Design and deploy personalization models and recommendation engines, translating business requirements into scalable, data-driven solutions.
Lead the full lifecycle of data science projects — from prototyping to production — ensuring alignment with enterprise platform standards.
Coordinate small teams of external developers, overseeing code quality, timelines, and deliverables.
Collaborate on cross-IT programs, managing dependencies with other teams (Data Platform, Integration, Digital, CRM) and business stakeholders.
Build and maintain scalable data pipelines on Databricks and Azure to support model training, serving, and continuous improvement.
Monitor model performance and proactively identify opportunities to enhance accuracy and business impact.
Contribute to ML Ops best practices and reusable frameworks within the Data Science area.

 

 

KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS

 

Master’s degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, Mathematics, Engineering) or equivalent experience.
4+ years of industry experience in data science, with a strong focus on personalization and recommendation systems.
Advanced proficiency in Python and PySpark; solid knowledge of SQL.
Hands-on experience with recommendation models (collaborative filtering, content-based, hybrid) and personalization algorithms at scale.
Strong experience with Databricks (MLflow, Feature Store, Model Serving) and Azure.
Familiarity with ML Ops practices (CI/CD for ML, model monitoring, automated retraining).
Experience managing external development teams and ensuring delivery quality.
Proven ability to work on complex, cross-functional programs in international environments.
Experience with Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) is preferred.
Previous experience in Fashion & Luxury or management consulting is a plus.
Excellent communication skills; ability to bridge technical and business language.
Fluency in Italian and English, written and spoken.

 

 

Joining our Company means working in a creative and international environment, with teams of people motivated by curiosity and the quest for excellence. The engine of our success is the importance and value that we place on the talent and passion of our people leading to their own professional growth.
Prada Group promotes an inclusive work environment, ensuring equal opportunities for all candidates, regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, or other personal characteristics. We believe that diversity is a value and we are committed to build a fair and respectful workplace for all.

Title: Científico/a de Datos / Machine Learning / Deep Learning
Description:
Senior Data Scientist – Machine Learning / Deep Learning | Madrid | Híbrido




En Indra Group, protegemos lo que más importa.

Somos una compañía global líder en tecnología y consultoría, presente en más de 140 países, que desarrolla soluciones innovadoras en sectores críticos como Defensa, Espacio y Transporte. Nuestro propósito es crear un futuro más seguro, eficiente y sostenible mediante la tecnología.




💻 ¡Únete a nuestro equipo de proyectos europeos en el área de Desarrollo Avanzado de Inteligencia Artificial! Trabajarás en iniciativas estratégicas aplicando modelos avanzados de machine learning, deep learning y tecnologías punteras para reforzar la capacidad tecnológica en entornos críticos.




💡 ¿Qué harás?

• Diseñar, desarrollar y optimizar modelos de machine learning y deep learning para diferentes casos de uso del proyecto.

• Implementar soluciones avanzadas en Python utilizando librerías fundamentales como NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn o SciPy.

• Entrenar, ajustar y evaluar arquitecturas neuronales (Transformers, RNN, LSTM, GRU) en frameworks como TensorFlow, PyTorch o Keras.• Aplicar técnicas de NLP con herramientas como Hugging Face Transformers y spaCy.

• Realizar fine-tuning de modelos de lenguaje (LLMs) como GPT, BERT y variantes.

• Participar en la integración de modelos dentro de plataformas MLOps (Kubeflow, BentoML).

• Colaborar en la contenedorización y despliegue de servicios mediante Docker y Kubernetes.

• Contribuir a actividades de investigación aplicada y experimentación en IA avanzada.

• Documentar procesos, resultados y conclusiones en informes técnicos.




🎯 ¿Qué buscamos en ti? (Requisitos imprescindibles)

✅ Titulación superior en informática, telecomunicaciones, ingeniería de software, física, matemáticas u otra ingeniería afín.

✅ Mínimo 3 años de experiencia como Data Scientist trabajando con machine learning o deep learning.

✅ Dominio avanzado de Python y librerías de ciencia de datos (NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn, SciPy).

✅ Experiencia con frameworks de deep learning como TensorFlow, PyTorch o Keras.

✅ Conocimientos de arquitecturas neuronales (Transformer, RNN, LSTM, GRU).

✅ Nivel de inglés alto.




No te preocupes si no cumples el 100% de lo que buscamos. ¡Anímate, conoce nuestra propuesta y dinos lo que nos puedes aportar! 🚀




Lo que te ofrecemos

• Estabilidad y Futuro 🏢✨: Proyectos a largo plazo en una empresa líder de Defensa

• Proyectos Innovadores y de Alto Alcance 🚀: Trabajarás con tecnologías de vanguardia, con un impacto tanto a nivel nacional como internacional.

• Ambiente Cercano y Transparente 🤝: Disfrutarás de una comunicación directa y fluida con responsables y compañeros, en un entorno colaborativo y abierto.

• Autonomía y Flexibilidad ⏳: conciliación real y adaptada a tu ritmo.

• Plan de carrera adaptado a ti 📈: Diseñado para impulsar tu crecimiento y desarrollo

• Formación continua 📚: en Open University y Udemy (¡más de 6.000 cursos para especializarte!).

• Descuentos exclusivos para tu bienestar 🎁: Ventajas en gimnasios, restaurantes, tiendas, ocio

• Retribución competitiva 💰 y planes de compensación flexibles 💸📊.




🕓 ¿Cómo es nuestro proceso de selección?

Revisión de tu perfil 🔍: Evaluamos tu experiencia y habilidades para determinar si encajas con lo que buscamos.
Primera toma de contacto (5-10 min) 📞: Si recibes una llamada de un número desconocido, ¡es nuestro equipo! Será una conversación rápida para conocerte y resolver cualquier duda.
Entrevista técnica 🤝: Te reunirás con el equipo, quienes te explicarán el proyecto y las tareas diarias. También exploraremos tus conocimientos técnicos. Además, te realizaremos breves pruebas de competencias psicológicas e inglés (si es necesario).

Senior — 4+ jaar ervaring → €5.000 – €5.500 p/m


📍 Regio Rotterdam | Hybride werken


Haystack People werkt exclusief samen met deze organisatie. Vragen of interesse? Stuur een DM.

Title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Abu Dhabi
Description:
🚀 Job title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer

📍 Location: Abu Dhabi (Full relocation support available)




We’re partnering with a leading organization in artificial intelligence to hire a Staff Machine Learning Engineer who will play a key role in building and scaling cutting-edge AI solutions.




🌟 About the Role

In this position, you will design, develop, deploy, and optimize machine learning models that power advanced AI systems. You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to turn complex business challenges into high-impact AI products.




🔧 Key Responsibilities

Model Development: Build, train, and deploy predictive models that enhance AI-driven solutions
Advanced AI Applications: Leverage state-of-the-art techniques including Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable ML solutions
Model Optimization: Continuously evaluate and improve model performance in real-world environments
Data Engineering: Design and maintain robust data pipelines to ensure high-quality inputs for ML systems




✅ Requirements

8+ years of experience in AI, Machine Learning, Reinforcement Learning, or Data Science
Master’s or Ph.D. in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
Strong expertise in Large Language Models and advanced fine-tuning techniques
Hands-on experience with ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or scikit-learn
Experience with Generative AI tools like LangChain, Weaviate, LangGraph, or LlamaIndex
Advanced programming skills in Python
Competitive programming or data science background (e.g., ACM/ICPC, IOI/NOI, Kaggle) is a strong plus

Title: Python Developer AI/ML
Description:
Job Description

Key-Biz S.r.l., società di consulenza ICT specializzata nella progettazione e realizzazione di soluzioni software, ricerca un/una Python Developer AI/ML da inserire presso un nostro cliente diretto operante in ambito sanitario, clinico e ricerca scientifica avanzata.

Il ruolo

La risorsa entrerà a far parte di un contesto altamente innovativo e orientato alla ricerca, collaborando allo sviluppo di soluzioni software avanzate a supporto di progetti in ambito clinico-medico e data science.

Il candidato lavorerà a stretto contatto con team multidisciplinari composti da sviluppatori, data scientist e figure tecnico-scientifiche, contribuendo alla progettazione e allo sviluppo di piattaforme intelligenti per l’elaborazione, l’integrazione e l’analisi dei dati clinici.

L’attività sarà focalizzata sullo sviluppo di applicazioni backend e soluzioni AI-based in Python, con utilizzo di framework e tecnologie moderne legate al mondo Machine Learning e AI Engineering.

Tra Le Principali Responsabilità:

Sviluppo e manutenzione di applicazioni backend in Python

Collaborazione diretta con team di Data Science per l’implementazione di modelli e pipeline AI/ML

Integrazione di modelli di intelligenza artificiale in contesti clinico-medici

Sviluppo di API e servizi per la gestione e l’elaborazione dei dati

Implementazione di workflow basati su LLM e orchestrazione tramite LangChain

Utilizzo del protocollo MCP per integrazione e interoperabilità tra servizi AI

Supporto all’industrializzazione di modelli sviluppati tramite PyTorch

Attività di debugging, testing, code review e ottimizzazione delle performance

Requisiti necessari

Laurea in Informatica, Ingegneria Informatica o discipline STEM affini

Esperienza consolidata nello sviluppo software con Python

Conoscenza di framework backend come Django o Flask

Esperienza o forte interesse verso ecosistemi AI/ML

Conoscenza di LangChain e architetture basate su LLM

Familiarità con PyTorch

Conoscenza del protocollo MCP (Model Context Protocol)

Familiarità con database relazionali e non relazionali

Attitudine al lavoro in team multidisciplinari e orientamento alla ricerca e innovazione

Modalità di lavoro

Ibrida: 3 giorni in presenza su Roma centro e 2 giorni da remoto.

Offerta

Contratto: Tempo indeterminato

RAL: €28.000 - €32.000

L’offerta è rivolta a candidati di entrambi i sessi (L.903/77).

Ai sensi del Regolamento (UE) 2016/679 (GDPR), i dati personali forniti dai candidati saranno trattati esclusivamente per finalità di selezione del personale, nel rispetto dei principi di liceità, correttezza e trasparenza.

Title: Pionjärroll inom AI- utveckling
Description:
Rollen:


Analysera befintliga arbetssätt, dataflöden och inmatningar i verksamhetens system
Identifiera möjligheter till förenkling, automatisering och optimering med hjälp av AI
Utveckla och testa AI‑mjukvara, främst i Python, kopplat till inbyggda eller tekniska systemmiljöer
Bidra till förbättrad datakvalitet, minskat manuellt arbete och ökad användarvänlighet
Delta i utvecklingsarbete kopplat till digitala stöd, informationshantering och systemintegrationer


Ska-krav:


Konsulten ska ha erfarenhet av att applicera AI i optimerings- och effektiviseringssyfte.
Konsulten ska ha goda kunskaper inom AI och inbyggda system.
Konsulten ska ha arbetslivserfarenhet där eget ansvar, självledarskap och samarbete i team varit centrala delar.
Konsulten ska ha goda kunskaper i Python.
Konsulten ska ha goda kunskaper inom AI kopplat till inbyggda system samt effektivisering och optimering.
Konsulten ska ha civilingenjörsexamen eller likvärdig utbildning inom relevant område.


Meriterande:


Konsulten bör ha dokumenterad erfarenhet av att utveckla AI-mjukvara.
Konsulten bör ha kunskap, erfarenhet och förmåga att utifrån uppdragets vision identifiera målbilden samt föreslå egna lösningar och applikationer.
Konsulten bör ha erfarenhet av att tillämpa AI i syfte att optimera och effektivisera verksamheter eller processer.


Var god förklara hur du uppfyller alla kraven.

Omfattning: 100%

Ort: Norrköping

Period: 03-08-2026-02-08-2027

Sista ansökningdag: 02-06-2026

Vi presenterar löpande. Om du är intresserad av ett uppdrag rekommenderar vi att du skickar in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.

Title: Manager Data Science & AI - Consulting
Description:
Manager Data Science & IA

CDI | Paris + télétravail (jusque 3 jours/semaines)

60-68K€ fixe + variable + intéréssement + avantages




Nous accompagnons un cabinet de conseil premium spécialisé exclusivement en Data et IA dans le recrutement d’un·e Manager Data Science & IA pour structurer et piloter leur expertise sur des projets à fort impact.

Il ne s’agit pas d’un rôle purement technique, ni d’un poste de pur manager. C’est un poste hybride, au cœur des enjeux clients, qui combine expertise data, posture de conseil et contribution au développement du cabinet.




🎯 Le contexte

Cabinet de plus de 150 consultants, reconnu pour la qualité de ses missions et son positionnement très pointu en Data & IA. Projets majoritairement au forfait, auprès de grands comptes multisectoriels. Culture d’innovation forte avec un Data Lab interne, production de POC IA, forte montée en compétences. Managers issus du terrain data, pas des profils commerciaux déconnectés des sujets.




💡 Le rôle

Vous intervenez comme référent sur les sujets Data Science et IA auprès des clients.
Vous contribuez aux réponses aux appels d’offres, au cadrage des besoins, au chiffrage et à la structuration des propositions.
Vous pilotez des missions complexes de bout en bout.
Vous accompagnez une équipe de consultants Data Scientist et IA dans leur progression.
Vous êtes force de proposition sur les sujets d’innovation, de structuration de l’offre et de positionnement du cabinet.




🧠 Profil recherché

3 à 7 ans d’expérience post diplôme.
Background Data Scientist, IA ou profil hybride data + business.
Maîtrise de Python et SQL, connaissance cloud appréciée.
Expérience en cabinet de conseil ou environnement similaire fortement souhaitée.
Habitude de travailler sur des projets au forfait, avec dimension avant vente, cadrage, structuration.
Très bonnes capacités de communication, posture conseil, capacité à interagir avec des interlocuteurs seniors côté client.
Management apprécié mais pas obligatoire si la posture est déjà solide.







Ce poste s’adresse à celles et ceux qui veulent rester proches du terrain, garder une vraie crédibilité data, tout en prenant progressivement une place plus stratégique dans les échanges clients.

Title: Junior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Company Description

Eleva Wellbeing is building a daily AI-supported mental wellbeing platform for working adults experiencing stress, anxiety and burnout. We combine daily check-ins, personalised wellbeing activities, validated screening tools such as PHQ-9 and GAD-7, and compassionate signposting when users may benefit from professional support.

We are a small founding team across product, clinical psychology and engineering. We care deeply about building something useful, safe, privacy-conscious and clinically responsible.




Role Description

We are looking for a Junior Machine Learning Engineer to help us build the foundations of Eleva Wellbeing's personalisation and AI systems.

This is a paid part-time, early-career role. It is suitable for someone who is already comfortable working with data and machine learning, and who wants to apply those skills to a real product with a meaningful mission.

You will work closely with Eleva Wellbeing's Co-Founder and CTO, with clinical input from our psychologist co-founder. You will not be expected to own production machine learning systems independently from day one. Instead, you will work on clearly scoped projects, receive mentorship, and gradually take more ownership as you grow.




What You Will Work On




Personalised activity recommendations

Help build and maintain Eleva's recommendation system: suggesting the right wellbeing activity based on a user's check-in, preferences, history and feedback.




Feedback loop and learning signals

Help define how Eleva learns from what worked: activity views, starts, completions, skips, ratings, and feeling changes before and after an activity.




Data and machine learning foundations

Support the foundations for future models: data exploration, feature definition, simple model experiments, evaluation metrics and documentation.




Safe AI exploration

Prototype carefully scoped AI use cases, such as personalised activity explanations, journaling reflection, or supportive check-in interactions, always with privacy, safety and clinical boundaries in mind.




What We Are Looking For




We are looking for someone early in their machine learning career, but already comfortable working with data, models and real product problems.




You do not need to have shipped large production machine learning systems before. But you should have built practical projects where you took data, explored it, tested an idea, evaluated the result and explained what you learned.




Required

1 to 3 years of experience in software, data or machine learning. Relevant university projects, internships, research projects, bootcamps or personal projects count.
Strong Python skills for data science workflows, including data exploration, feature engineering, experimentation and model prototyping.
Practical experience with tools such as pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn or notebooks.
Basic SQL skills and comfort working with structured data.
Understanding of core machine learning concepts such as features, training and test data, evaluation metrics, overfitting and model validation.
Interest or hands-on experience in recommendation systems, ranking, personalisation, user behaviour or predictive modelling.
Ability to think about how Eleva should learn from users over time: what was recommended, what the user did next, whether they completed it, and whether it helped.
Good engineering habits: Git, clean code, testing, clear documentation and good communication.
Care for privacy, responsible AI and the sensitivity of working with mental health data.
Comfortable working remotely, independently and with guidance in an early-stage startup.




You will stand out if you have

Built a recommendation, ranking, personalisation or “next best action” project.
Worked with product data, user journeys, event tracking or behavioural datasets.
Designed or analysed signals such as views, starts, completions, skips, ratings, or before-and-after outcomes.
Built a machine learning project end to end, from data preparation to evaluation and documented results.
Experimented with large language models, retrieval, embeddings, prompt evaluation or AI safety.
Worked on healthcare, mental health, education or another product involving sensitive data.




Nice to have

Experience with contextual bandits, reinforcement learning, learning to rank or experimentation.
Familiarity with PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost, LightGBM or similar tools.
Familiarity with Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Vertex AI, Airflow, MLflow or similar tools.
Interest in building machine learning features that can eventually be used in a real product.
Interest in psychology, behaviour change, CBT, ACT, digital wellbeing or clinical safety.
Fluency in Portuguese.




What You Will Get

Paid part-time experience on a real AI-enabled mental wellbeing product.
Direct mentorship from an experienced CTO.
Exposure to recommendation systems, feedback loops, machine learning pipelines, product analytics and AI safety.
Clinical context from a practising psychologist co-founder.
A meaningful product mission: helping people manage stress, anxiety and burnout.
A potential pathway to a larger machine learning engineering role as Eleva Wellbeing grows.




Role Details

Title: Junior Machine Learning Engineer
Focus: Personalisation, recommendations and AI
Type: Paid part-time internship
Hours: Flexible, ideally 10 to 20 hours per week
Location: Remote-first, Europe timezone preferred
Preferred hubs: Lisbon, Berlin or Zürich
Supervision: Directly supervised by the CTO
Start: As soon as possible
Compensation: Paid hourly or fixed monthly part-time compensation, depending on experience and availability.




How to Apply

Directly via LinkedIn - Easy Apply.




If you are curious, thoughtful and eager to grow into this area, we would love to hear from you.

  • Experience with contextual bandits, reinforcement learning, learning to rank or experimentation.
  • Familiarity with PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost, LightGBM or similar tools.
  • Familiarity with Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Vertex AI, Airflow, MLflow or similar tools.
  • Interest in building machine learning features that can eventually be used in a real product.
  • Interest in psychology, behaviour change, CBT, ACT, digital wellbeing or clinical safety.
  • Fluency in Portuguese.


What You Will Get

  • Paid part-time experience on a real AI-enabled mental wellbeing product.
  • Direct mentorship from an experienced CTO.
  • Exposure to recommendation systems, feedback loops, machine learning pipelines, product analytics and AI safety.
  • Clinical context from a practising psychologist co-founder.
  • A meaningful product mission: helping people manage stress, anxiety and burnout.
  • A potential pathway to a larger machine learning engineering role as Eleva Wellbeing grows.


Role Details

  • Title: Junior Machine Learning Engineer
  • Focus: Personalisation, recommendations and AI
  • Type: Paid part-time internship
  • Hours: Flexible, ideally 10 to 20 hours per week
  • Location: Remote-first, Europe timezone preferred
  • Preferred hubs: Lisbon, Berlin or Zürich
  • Supervision: Directly supervised by the CTO
  • Start: As soon as possible
  • Compensation: Paid hourly or fixed monthly part-time compensation, depending on experience and availability.


How to Apply

Directly via LinkedIn - Easy Apply.


If you are curious, thoughtful and eager to grow into this area, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Data scientist I
Description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Data scientist I

Who is Mastercard?

Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.

Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.

Overview

Are you an experienced data scientist with a passion for technological innovation, have a can-do attitude and strong sense of ownership? The Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) platform is the perfect opportunity for you to assist in the design & development of a world-class unified platform, enabling Small and Medium Enterprises globally.

The SME team is looking for a junior data scientist to work in development of machine learning models and business intelligence projects. You will also work closely with cross-functional teams, including data engineers, data analysts, and software engineers, to drive data-driven decision making throughout the organization.

Key Responsibilities


Aggregates and collates client data sets and conducts data manipulation and technical analysis leveraging industry techniques and best practices to identify trends and draw insights
Cleans and structures data sets fed to downstream analytics
Assists with the development and validation of dashboards and reports for client/ stakeholder projects
Escalates technical errors or bugs detected in project work
Assists with ad hoc work as delegated by senior level colleagues



All About You


Highly motivated and detail-oriented individual with a passion for data analysis and problem-solving.
Skilled in aggregating and collating client data sets, conducting thorough data manipulation and technical analysis using industry-standard techniques and best practices.
Experienced in assisting with the development and validation of dashboards and reports for client and stakeholder projects.
Proactive attitude with a willingness to assist with ad hoc tasks as delegated by senior colleagues.



Corporate Security Responsibility

Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security. All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And Therefore, It Is Expected That The Successful Candidate For This Position Must:


Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.



Corporate Security Responsibility

All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must:


Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
The Role

We’re partnering with an innovative HealthTech company building intelligent AI-driven solutions that improve healthcare delivery, operational efficiency, and patient outcomes. They are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to develop and deploy scalable ML solutions.




What You’ll Be Doing

Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models for healthcare applications including predictive analytics, anomaly detection, medical imaging, patient risk modelling, and intelligent automation
Build scalable ML pipelines supporting model training, validation, monitoring, and real-time inference in clinical and healthcare environments
Develop robust preprocessing and feature engineering workflows for healthcare, patient, and IoT/wearable device data
Collaborate with software engineers, data teams, product specialists, and healthcare stakeholders to integrate ML solutions into production platforms
Contribute to AI-powered perception and analytics systems using technologies such as medical imaging, sensor data, and real-time monitoring devices




What They’re Looking For

2–6+ years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or related roles
Strong Python programming skills
Experience with ML/DL frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Scikit-learn
Proven experience building and deploying ML pipelines in production environments
Exposure to healthcare, medical data, wearable technology, or regulated environments is highly advantageous
Familiarity with data engineering concepts including ETL/ELT workflows, streaming pipelines, and sensor or patient data processing
Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP




What’s on Offer

Hybrid working environment with offices based in Ghent
Opportunity to work on innovative HealthTech products with meaningful real-world impact
Strong focus on technical growth, experimentation, and applied AI research
Competitive salary package up to €6,000/month depending on experience
Company car or mobility package included
13+ additional ADV days
Clear progression opportunities into senior or lead-level positions

Title: Data Scientist - QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

As a Data Scientist, you will collaborate with clients and interdisciplinary teams to develop impactful analytics solutions, optimize code, and solve real-world business problems across diverse industries. You’ll grow as a technologist by contributing to cutting-edge projects, R&D, and global conferences while working alongside world-class talent in a dynamic, innovative environment.

In this role, you will partner with clients to understand their needs and develop impactful analytics solutions. You will translate business problems into analytical challenges, build models to solve them, and ensure they are evaluated with relevant metrics. You’ll also contribute to internal tools, participate in R&D projects, and have opportunities to attend and present at conferences like NIPS and ICML.

Your work will create real-world impact. By identifying patterns in data and delivering innovative solutions, you will help clients maintain their competitive advantage and transform their day-to-day operations. Your contributions will directly influence business outcomes and drive lasting improvements across industries.

You will be in Amsterdam or Brussels, and collaborate closely with data scientists, data engineers, machine learning engineers, designers, and product managers around the world working on interdisciplinary projects that use math, statistics, and machine learning to derive insights from raw data. You will help global companies transform their businesses and enhance performance across industries such as healthcare, automotive, energy, and elite sports.

At McKinsey, you’ll thrive in an unparalleled environment for growth. You’ll develop a sought-after perspective by connecting technology and business value, tackle real-life challenges across diverse industries, and collaborate with inspiring multidisciplinary teams, gaining a holistic understanding of AI and its potential to drive transformation.

Our Tech Stack

While we advocate for using the right tech for the right task, we often leverage the following technologies: Python, PySpark, the PyData stack, SQL, Airflow, Databricks, our own open-source data pipelining framework called Kedro, Dask/RAPIDS, container technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, cloud solutions such as AWS, GCP, and Azure, and more.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Bachelors, Masters or PhD level in a discipline such as: computer science, machine learning, applied statistics, mathematics, engineering or artificial intelligence.
0-2+ years of professional experience in applying machine learning and data mining techniques to real problems with copious amounts of data
Programming experience (focus on machine learning): R and/or Python (must), SPSS, SAS, Ruby, Hadoop (valued).
Ability to prototype statistical analysis and modeling algorithms and apply these algorithms for data driven solutions to problems in new domains
Ability to independently own and drive model development, balancing demands and deadlines.
Demonstrated aptitude for analytics.
While we advocate for using the right tech for the right task, we often leverage the following technologies: Python, PySpark, the PyData stack, SQL, Airflow, Databricks, our own open-source data pipelining framework called Kedro, Dask/RAPIDS, container technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes, cloud solutions such as AWS, GCP, and Azure, and more.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, in English, with the ability to adjust your style to suit different perspectives and seniority levels. Knowledge of French or Dutch is valued.
Exceptional time management to meet your responsibilities in a complex and largely autonomous work environment.
Good presentation and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex analytical concepts to people from other fields.
Willingness to travel.
